description: A tiny scalar-valued autograd engine in ~100 lines + a neural net library on top

key_decisions.reasoning: Tensors are just batched scalars. If you understand the scalar case completely, tensors are just an efficiency detail.

key_decisions.decision: Build the backward pass manually for each operation
key_decisions.reasoning: This forces you to derive every gradient yourself. There's no shortcut to understanding backprop — you have to do it.
PyTorch autograd is ~500k lines. But the core idea fits in 100. Separate the idea from the engineering.

lessons: Backpropagation is just the chain rule applied recursively — nothing more
Build the smallest possible thing that captures the core idea
If you can implement it yourself you actually understand it
